defences.name: Self-defence (Private Defence)
defences.description: Under BNS Section 37, the right of private defence of the body extends to causing death if there is reasonable apprehension of death or grievous hurt.
defences.applicability: When the accused acted to protect themselves or others from imminent deadly harm.

defences.name: Grave and sudden provocation
defences.description: If the act was committed in the heat of passion caused by grave and sudden provocation, it may reduce the charge to culpable homicide (BNS-101).
defences.applicability: When the victim provoked the accused severely and immediately before the act.

defences.name: Accident/Misfortune
defences.description: Under general exceptions, an act done by accident without criminal intention or knowledge is not an offence.
defences.applicability: When death occurred accidentally during a lawful act done lawfully.

defences.name: Alibi
defences.description: Proving the accused was at a different location at the time of the offence.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence placing the accused elsewhere.

defences.name: Insanity (Unsoundness of mind)
defences.description: Under BNS Section 22, nothing is an offence done by a person of unsound mind.
defences.applicability: When the accused was incapable of understanding the nature of the act.

strategies.scenario: Accused of voluntarily causing hurt (simple assault)

defences.name: Self-defence
defences.description: Right of private defence under BNS Section 37 permits use of reasonable force to protect oneself.
defences.applicability: When the accused was protecting themselves from an attack.

defences.name: Consent
defences.description: If the hurt was caused with the victim's consent, it is not an offence (for persons above 18).
defences.applicability: Sports injuries, consensual physical activities.

defences.name: Grave and sudden provocation
defences.description: The victim severely provoked the accused immediately before the incident.
defences.applicability: When there was immediate provocation.

defences.name: False accusation
defences.description: Challenge the evidence and establish that the complaint is false or exaggerated.
defences.applicability: When medical evidence does not support the claims.

strategies.scenario: Accused of voluntarily causing grievous hurt

defences.name: Self-defence
defences.description: Right of private defence permits causing grievous hurt if there was reasonable apprehension of grievous hurt to oneself.
defences.applicability: When the accused faced threat of serious injury.

defences.name: Accident
defences.description: The injury was caused accidentally without criminal intention.
defences.applicability: When the harm was unintentional.

defences.name: Disproportionate medical report
defences.description: Challenge the medical evidence and classification of injuries as 'grievous'.
defences.applicability: When injuries may not meet the legal definition of grievous hurt.

defences.name: Right of private defence of property
defences.description: The accused believed in good faith that they had a right to the property.
defences.applicability: When there is a genuine dispute over ownership.

defences.name: Claim of right
defences.description: The accused took the property under a bona fide claim of right.
defences.applicability: When the accused genuinely believed the property belonged to them.

defences.name: Consent
defences.description: The owner consented to the taking of property.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of permission.

defences.name: No dishonest intention
defences.description: The taking was without dishonest intention (e.g., borrowing with intent to return).
defences.applicability: When the accused intended to return the property.

defences.name: No force or threat used
defences.description: If no force or fear was used, the charge should be reduced to theft.
defences.applicability: When the prosecution cannot prove use of force or threat.

defences.name: Alibi
defences.description: The accused was not present at the scene.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of being elsewhere.

defences.name: Mistaken identity
defences.description: The victim incorrectly identified the accused.
defences.applicability: In cases of fleeting encounters or poor visibility.

defences.name: Self-defence
defences.description: The accused was protecting their own property.
defences.applicability: When the accused had rightful claim to the property.

defences.name: No deception
defences.description: The accused made honest representations and did not deceive anyone.
defences.applicability: When representations were truthful or based on genuine belief.

defences.name: No dishonest intention
defences.description: The accused did not have fraudulent or dishonest intent at the time.
defences.applicability: When the failure was due to genuine business failure, not fraud.

defences.name: Civil dispute
defences.description: The matter is essentially a civil dispute over a contract, not criminal cheating.
defences.applicability: When the dispute arises from a genuine business relationship.

defences.name: Consent / awareness
defences.description: The complainant was fully aware of the risks involved.
defences.applicability: When the complainant voluntarily entered into the arrangement.

strategies.scenario: Accused of criminal breach of trust

defences.name: No entrustment
defences.description: The property was never entrusted to the accused.
defences.applicability: When the prosecution cannot prove entrustment.

defences.name: No dishonest misappropriation
defences.description: The property was used for its intended purpose.
defences.applicability: When funds were used for legitimate purposes.

defences.name: Civil liability only
defences.description: The breach is a civil matter, not criminal.
defences.applicability: When there is no criminal intent, only a commercial dispute.

strategies.scenario: Accused under domestic violence / cruelty by husband (498A equivalent)

defences.name: No cruelty established
defences.description: The acts alleged do not constitute 'cruelty' as defined in law.
defences.applicability: When the allegations are about normal marital disagreements.

defences.name: False complaint / misuse
defences.description: The Supreme Court has noted misuse of 498A; establish that the complaint is filed with ulterior motive.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of false accusations.

defences.name: Compromise / mediation
defences.description: Seek resolution through mediation; Supreme Court has allowed quashing of 498A in cases of genuine compromise.
defences.applicability: When both parties are willing to reconcile.

defences.name: No dowry demand
defences.description: Establish that no demand for dowry was ever made.
defences.applicability: When dowry-related allegations are part of the complaint.

strategies.scenario: Accused of causing death by negligence (road accident)

defences.name: Contributory negligence
defences.description: The victim also contributed to the accident through their own negligence.
defences.applicability: When the victim was jaywalking, drunk, etc.

defences.name: No rashness or negligence
defences.description: The accused was driving within speed limits and following traffic rules.
defences.applicability: When the accident was unavoidable.

defences.name: Mechanical failure
defences.description: The accident was caused by vehicle malfunction, not driver negligence.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of vehicle defect.

defences.name: Sudden emergency
defences.description: The accused faced a sudden emergency and acted reasonably.
defences.applicability: When another vehicle or obstacle caused the situation.

defences.name: Composite negligence
defences.description: Multiple parties contributed to the accident.
defences.applicability: When other drivers or road conditions contributed.

defences.name: No threat made
defences.description: The accused did not make any threat.
defences.applicability: When there is no evidence of threatening communication.

defences.name: Exercise of legal right
defences.description: The accused was merely asserting a legal right (e.g., demanding repayment of a loan).
defences.applicability: When the demand was for something legally owed.

defences.name: No intent to intimidate
defences.description: The words used were not intended to cause alarm.
defences.applicability: When the communication was misinterpreted.

defences.name: Right of way / easement
defences.description: The accused had a legal right of way or easement.
defences.applicability: When there is a documented right of access.

defences.name: No criminal intent
defences.description: The entry was without intent to commit an offence or intimidate.
defences.applicability: When the entry was accidental or innocent.

defences.name: Ownership / right to possession
defences.description: The accused has a legal right to the property.
defences.applicability: When there is a property ownership dispute.

defences.name: Authorization
defences.description: The accused had authority to sign or create the document.
defences.applicability: When there is a power of attorney or authorization.

defences.name: No intent to defraud
defences.description: The document was not made with intent to cause damage or commit fraud.
defences.applicability: When the document was made in good faith.

defences.name: Genuine document
defences.description: The document is genuine, not forged.
defences.applicability: When expert analysis supports authenticity.

defences.name: Truth as defence
defences.description: Under the First Exception, it is not defamation if the imputation is true and in the public interest.
defences.applicability: When the statement is factually accurate.

defences.name: Good faith opinion
defences.description: Fair comment on a matter of public interest made in good faith.
defences.applicability: When commenting on public figures or public matters.

defences.name: Privileged communication
defences.description: Communications in judicial proceedings, to public servants, or in other privileged contexts.
defences.applicability: When the statement was made in a protected context.

strategies.scenario: Accused of assault on woman with intent to outrage modesty

defences.name: No intent to outrage modesty
defences.description: The contact was accidental with no intention to outrage modesty.
defences.applicability: Accidental contact in crowded places.

defences.name: False complaint
defences.description: The complaint is motivated by ulterior motive.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of false accusation.

defences.name: Alibi
defences.description: The accused was not present at the alleged time and place.
defences.applicability: When there is evidence of being elsewhere.

defences.name: No repeated contact
defences.description: There was no repeated following or contacting.
defences.applicability: When the alleged incidents were isolated.

defences.name: Legitimate purpose
defences.description: The contact was for a legitimate purpose (e.g., professional, debt collection).
defences.applicability: When there was a valid reason for contact.

defences.name: No clear indication of disinterest
defences.description: The complainant did not clearly communicate disinterest.
defences.applicability: When the accused reasonably believed contact was welcome.

defences.name: Consent of the person
defences.description: The person went willingly (applicable if above the age of consent).
defences.applicability: When the person is an adult who consented.

defences.name: Lawful guardianship
defences.description: The accused is the lawful guardian of the person.
defences.applicability: In custody disputes where accused is a parent.

defences.name: No taking or enticement
defences.description: The accused did not take or entice the person.
defences.applicability: When the person came voluntarily.

defences.name: No intention to kill
defences.description: The accused did not have the intention to cause death.
defences.applicability: When the injury was not aimed at vital body parts.

defences.name: Self-defence
defences.description: The accused acted in self-defence against a deadly attack.
defences.applicability: When the accused faced a threat to their life.

defences.name: Charge should be reduced
defences.description: The facts support a lesser charge (e.g., voluntarily causing hurt, not attempt to murder).
defences.applicability: When the nature of injuries suggests lower intent.

defences.name: No intent to cause damage
defences.description: The damage was accidental, not intentional.
defences.applicability: When the damage occurred unintentionally.

defences.name: Right to property
defences.description: The accused had a right to the property.
defences.applicability: When the accused owns or co-owns the property.

defences.name: Trivial damage
defences.description: The damage was so trivial that no reasonable person would complain.
defences.applicability: When the damage is negligible.

defences.name: Claim of right
defences.description: The accused took the property under a bona fide claim of right.
defences.applicability: When the accused believed they had a right to the property.

defences.name: No dishonest intention
defences.description: The property was kept safely with intent to return.
defences.applicability: When the property was found and the accused tried to locate the owner.

defences.name: Death from natural causes
defences.description: Establish that death occurred from natural causes or illness, not burns or injury.
defences.applicability: When medical evidence supports natural death.

defences.name: No cruelty or harassment
defences.description: There was no cruelty or harassment before death.
defences.applicability: When witnesses support a normal marital relationship.

defences.name: No dowry demand
defences.description: No dowry was demanded from the deceased or her family.
defences.applicability: When there is no evidence of dowry demand.

defences.name: Death after 7 years of marriage
defences.description: The presumption under Section 103 only applies within 7 years of marriage.
defences.applicability: When the death occurred more than 7 years after marriage.
